stories.title: China's Moonshot pauses Kimi subscriptions amid hot demand, IPO push
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese AI chatbot company called Kimi paused letting people buy subscriptions because so many people want to use it. They're also preparing to sell shares of the company to the public.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in China — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: How much energy do data centers and artificial intelligence use?
stories.simple_summary: A news article looked at how much electricity data centers (huge computer buildings that power AI) and AI systems use.
stories.why_it_matters: This helps you understand that AI uses real electricity and has real costs, which eventually gets passed along in prices people pay.

stories.title: Are Trump and AI driving a nuclear power boom? : Short Wave
stories.simple_summary: Some people think President Trump and AI interest are leading to more nuclear power plants being built.
stories.why_it_matters: If nuclear power grows, it could eventually affect your electricity bills or energy costs, though this is still mostly future talk.

stories.title: AI-altered images on birdwatching forums putting research at risk
stories.simple_summary: People are using AI to fake or alter bird photos on birdwatching websites, which makes real bird research harder because scientists can't tell which photos are real.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows that AI can create convincing fake images, which means you should be careful about believing pictures you see online without checking the source.

stories.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
stories.simple_summary: Jensen Huang, who runs a major AI computer chip company, visited Japan and made business deals with Japanese technology companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI (the company behind ChatGPT), and people are wondering if this lawsuit could stop OpenAI from making AI hardware devices.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal battle between tech giants; for now, it mainly affects investors and won't change what you can do with AI.

stories.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Just Showed the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Build-Out Is Alive and Well With This Jaw-Dropping Announcement
stories.simple_summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company announced something important about making chips for AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors in tech stocks — the headline doesn't give enough detail to explain the real impact.

stories.title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
stories.simple_summary: Someone wrote that a song created by an AI music tool called Suno is actually pretty good, even though they usually find AI music boring.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI music-making is improving, though it's still mostly a curiosity — most people still prefer music made by human musicians.

stories.title: Here Are My Top 3 Artificial Intelligence Stocks to Buy Right Now
stories.simple_summary: An investment newsletter recommended three AI company stocks to buy.
stories.why_it_matters: This is investment advice for people who trade stocks — not relevant unless you're actively buying and selling company shares.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
stories.simple_summary: Filmmaker Christopher Nolan said AI is like a Trojan horse (a hidden danger disguised as something helpful).
stories.why_it_matters: This is one director's opinion about AI risks — it's worth thinking about, but it's an artistic viewpoint, not a concrete warning about something specific.

stories.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
stories.simple_summary: A nonprofit organization is building AI tools that they want to make free and available to everyone worldwide, not just wealthy countries.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually mean more people around the world get access to AI tools for free, though it's still in early stages.

stories.title: Step Into the ‘Zone of Genius’ (Before A.I. Takes Your Job)
stories.simple_summary: A New York Times article discusses finding your best talents and skills before AI changes the job market.
stories.why_it_matters: This is general advice to think about what unique skills you have that AI can't easily replace — good to consider as technology changes.
